title: CXXRTL: internal assertion on reduced testcase
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Repro files: [hazard3-alu-cxxrtl-repro.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/7717168/hazard3-alu-cxxrtl-repro.zip)

This zip file contains two directories. Each directory is self-contained, with source and `.ys` files.

* `verilog_src` -- part of the Verilog hierarchy of my project, bisected as far as possible (the ALU and two subordinate modules)
* `ilang_reduced` -- the above, reduced with bugpoint _as far as possible_. I had some issues here with bugpoint not terminating

`cd` into either directory, and run 

```
yosys bug.ys
```

I'm on `Yosys 0.12+23 (git sha1 5e5c8a54c, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)`, which was `master` on Monday. You should see:

```
ERROR: Assert '!is_lhs' failed in backends/cxxrtl/cxxrtl_backend.cc:965.
```

## Expected behavior

Produce a C++ output, or a diagnostic saying an issue with the Verilog that I can fix

## Actual behavior

```
ERROR: Assert '!is_lhs' failed in backends/cxxrtl/cxxrtl_backend.cc:965.
```

## Further Troubleshooting

I was previously on Yosys version `c2afcbe7`, which did not trip this same assertion, and as far as I can tell produced working build output. I have gone back to rebuild that version of Yosys, and run it on the repro zip above, and it does not trip this assertion.

`c2afcbe7` was a few commits after the fix for #2882, which was in fact the same assertion tripping on an earlier version of this same ALU.

I tried to reduce the testcase from the full source of my project, and found that bugpoint didn't terminate, even when left running overnight. Eventually Yosys was killed. After I bisected down to a smaller part of my project source, I _still_ found that bugpoint would not terminate. It continually "finds something". In the reduced testcase above I see a lot of wires called `$delete_wire$xxxx` and I wonder if bugpoint is somehow looping on deleting its own created wires. I'll open another issue for this.

I reduced as far as I could by hacking `bugpoint` to output its last successful testcase after hitting 20 000 iterations.
